title: mildly cheesy korean eggs jjimdak-style fried rice bowl
description: a comforting and aromatic korean rice bowl dish loaded with eggs, deep-fried tofu, and a rich, cheesy sauce. this version is inspired by jeolla province's signature jjimdak.
instructions: heat coconut oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.
add the sliced onion and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until translucent.
add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
add the gochujang, gochugaru, and fish sauce (if using), and stir to combine.
add the cubed tofu and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until lightly browned.
add the cooked white rice to the pan and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes.
crack in the eggs and stir-fry until just set.
add the cheese and stir-fry until melted and creamy.
transfer the fried rice mixture to a serving dish and top with a sprinkle of green onion.
drizzle with sesame oil and serve immediately.
to assemble the dish, place a scoop of the fried rice mixture into a bowl, then top with additional green onion and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds.
